Hi! I'm Lindy.

I design and build technologies to support social-emotional connection.

My research focus is on Human-Centered AI to promote inclusion and sustainable structural change. I am particularly interested in leveraging Affective Computing and Social Computing to facilitate mindfulness, compassion, and dialogue. I am currently a product manager at Microsoft, where I collaborate with Microsoft Research's Human Understanding and Empathy (HUE) group to research empathic AI and emotional well-being. My approach to Human-Computer Interaction is transdisciplinary, inspired by neurodiversity and trauma-informed care. Influenced by Critical Computing and Social Justice, I am committed to conducting community-based participatory action research that centers marginalized voices and intersectionality.

Previously, I have built assistive technology at Microsoft for people with visual, mobility, and cognitive disabilities. In my free time, I love making art through needlework (e.g. knitting, crochet), music (e.g. ocarina, ukuele), and dance (e.g. aerial acrobatics, ballroom).

Let's Chat!

Publications

Pranav Khadpe, Lindy Le, Kate Nowak, Shamsi Iqbal, & Jina Suh. DISCERN: Designing Representation Material to Represent What Matters in Organizational Decision-Making. (Under Review)

Connor Lawless, Jakob Schoeffer, Lindy Le, Kael Rowan, Shilad Sen, Cristina St Hill, Jina Suh, & Bahareh Sarrafzadeh. Enabling Conversational Preference Elicitation using Large Language Models and Constraint Programming. (In Submission)
